comments: Show files below wrongly dated: Tommy refers to the East Anglian Rock '81 festival on the 20th September, "this coming Sunday." TV also mentions that it has been 11 years since Jimi Hendrix died (18 September 1970).

sessions.details: Recorded 1981-07-10. First TX 24 July 1981. No known commercial release.

track_listing.artist: Bob Seger & The Silver Bullet Band

transcript.text: Well, hello there. This is TV on the Radio, here Thomas the Vance, and welcome once again to The Friday Rock Show.
Tonight, to catch up as fast as possible on the latest releases, we actually concentrate on the newer LPs.
But we also repeat our recent session by the band called Myagra, and second time around, it sounds even better.
Now, the groups and the singers you'll hear tonight on the program are The Rods, Michael Schenker, Genesis, The New King Crimson, Gary Moore and Friends, Greg Lake, Bob Seger, Cozy Powell,
powell tangerine dream saxon and girl among others we've got the answer to the fighting
like connection of a fortnight ago and a new one but tonight we start with this that's a new
american band a three-piece band out of the united states of america the album's available
on import on arista records the band are called the rods as indeed is the lp and the track is
called rock hard and before that something by death leopard from their album which i feel has
gone by the board in this country it seems not to have done as well as i think it should have the
album is called high and dry the track you heard was no no no it's 11 minutes after 10 on the
friday rock show like to say hello to a gentleman by the name of trevor who comes from luton or
thereabouts according to carol who lives in hena in derbyshire it seems they met up at donnington
she remembers him fondly alistair and nigel who are a couple of twins have a birthday today a
17th birthday a joint one mum and dad kitchen of 11 cornwall road retford and not say hello to both
And happy birthday, but I also mention the good friends of the Kitchen family, i.e. Ada, and the two Steves, and Ivan and Adrian Pires as well.
I've got a card from Germany that says when I say hello to a lady by the name of Susan, who's 16 today.
The card came from somewhere around Hamelin in Germany, from a fellow by the name of Chris Walker.
Sue, happy birthday.
And to Sharon and Steve Todd, may I say that obviously you're both very much in love and that your son, I trust, gets better soon.
sound exactly what it is just edited highlights that's the album version of abacab by genesis
from the album of the same name which is really an excellent album when i first heard it i thought
some tracks are really oh gotta get hold of them other tracks leave behind but when you hear it
again and again and again you realize just how sophisticated and how clever banks collins and
rutherford really are and before that something from the michael shenker group from their album
msg the track was called but i want more want to see them the other night had a lovely time some
people were saying that they thought the songs weren't that strong but i i don't know i just
remember having a great night watching them 35 minutes after 10 it is a lady or rather a
gentleman by the name of what is it mckinney's i don't know his first name is 19 today and he lives
up there in livingston his sister sends him best wishes and also wants me to send her love to her
her boyfriend david and say hello to lynn and ronald helen and crawford freebird honeychild
ocean gypsy jill mcleod richard o'donnell jimmy and morag john mccalevey i think it is david
mackie as well i also say hello to curly top the mule skinner graham neil and lynn if they're
listening to this channel and i hope that they are let me hear from carol knight who lives in
leicester dear tom could you please play a record for the people i met at reading rock festival over
the bank holiday weekend to ginger from odly in leicestershire to phil and leaf from birmingham
and especially to Martin from somewhere near Gillingham in Kent.
Did you tell Martin I enjoyed the pleasure of his company?
And I look forward to seeing him at Reading next year.
King Crimson have reformed, and this is how they sound.
In 1974, from their albums Starless and Bible Black,
that track was called The Great Deceiver.
In 1974, in the band were Frippin' Bruford and David Cross and John Whetton.
And before that, you heard the title track from the new, reformed,
if you like, King Crimson.
The album is called Discipline, as indeed was the track.
In the band now are Adrian Beeloo and Mr. Fripp himself,
Tony Levin and Bill Bruford.
Levin and Beeloo are American,
Fripp and Bruford are 100% United Kingdom.
They're going to be going on tour shortly in this country.
The album, incidentally, is out on EG Records.
And Mr. Bruford is going to be on Rock On next Saturday.
That flamboyant character's death, Mark Bolan and Jeepster,
it's just been re-released as a single.
On the other side, you'll find Get It On,
And before that, our first track from our repeat session by a band called Niagara.
The track you heard performed by them was called Crossfire.
It's 16 and a half minutes to 11.
Quick mention here to a gentleman who had a birthday on the 12th of this month.
That gentleman is actually a lady by the name of Janet Pickup.
Sorry, Janet.
Also wants to say hello to all the Rochdale Edbangers, especially Woody, underlined, Kel, Jimmy and Eddie.
Steve Bumstead is having a birthday on the 22nd of September.
Joe Bumstead says hello
Joe is his sister
But I say hello to all the
Al Woodley headbangers
Maz, Jill, Sue, Piggy Eyes
I think it is, Mish and James and Mark
and Patty and Yvonne
Thank you very much indeed, lots of love
Fluff, know him well, friend of mine
Little card here, please please would you send a message
to a guy I met at Reading Rock Festival
on this Friday's rock show
as I know no other way of telling him
Ian, I'll always remember
love from peasant
I'm going to suss out the postmark, Alton there.
John and Elaine live in Pitfordles, Cults in Aberdeen.
And they are currently travelling the highlands of Scotland.
They wanted to hear some decent music.
But I also mention the brother of said people,
who is also in Aberdeen and likes rock music.
OK, I'm going to play you two pieces of music now
which are exactly the same song.
I'll set it up for you and tell you the story.
The first version you're going to hear
is going to be released as a single,
Rush released as a single next week.
It's by Gary Moore and Friends,
and on this recording his friends are
Tommy Aldrich, who's now with the Ozzy Osbourne band,
Charlie Hume, who formerly was with Ted Nugent,
and Jimmy Bain, who at one time was with Wild Horses.
I never know whether he's in that band or out of it.
And, of course, Mr. Gary Moore as well.
The song is called Nuclear Attack.
Then I'm going to play you a version which is going to be included
on the new album by Greg Lake,
which we actually played this track about three weeks ago.
just for fun you can compare the two so first gary moore and friends and then greg lake and
his band which of course includes mr gary moore and nuclear attack same song two different versions
but with the same lead guitarist the latter version was by the greg lake band it's included
on their new album which is coming out soon the track's called nuclear attack and before that
something that's coming out as a single next week by gary moore and friends nuclear attack
Both versions different, same chord structure, but both, I think, just as good as each other.
Four and a half minutes it is before 11.
Here's a little mention to a couple of people up there in Dundee.
To Linda and Jim Grogan, best wishes from Rick and Joe Murray, whom I met quite recently on the Rock of Gibraltar.
I have a card that says, dear TV, a group of headbangers from Torbay in Devon went to Haddocket, Yorkshire for a holiday during August.
While they were there, they met a young lady from Preston in Lancashire, and we had the time of our lives.
now 276 miles apart it's up to you to re-establish contact please say hello to
hazel suzuki whitmore from nat the clutch i bet hazel knows all about that also to rachel and
pam and gareth i love black santa davis and a kevin yamaha gotta mention helen gone it says here
who lives at 64 broadway i think it's kettley or possibly oh it's definitely kettley because
This is up by Telford and Schwarzschild.
Anthony Newman of Newport in Gwent says hello.
And now here's something that's old but new
in as much as it's just been released on a compilation album.
It's my budgie.
Here's the ideal combination.
A great vocalist, some amazing musicians,
an ace producer by the name of Tony Wilson,
and a sound engineer who knows what he's doing called Dave Dade.
Second number from our repeat session by Niagara.
Magic to my ears.
And before that, something from an album called The Best of Budgie.
It's just been released on Cube Records.
The track was called Homicidal Suicidal.
Nine minutes after 11, the Friday Night Connection.
The one we set a fortnight ago, well, we've got the answer here
from a gentleman by the name of Carl Noonan.
Carl lives at 28 Bankfield, Digmore, Skelmersdale, Lancashire.
But you knew that anyway, didn't you, Carl?
Carl wrote, Dear Tom, the answer to last week's Friday Night Connection was
track one, Never Been Any Reason,
by Head East from the album Flat as a Pancake.
Track two, Rock Candy, Montrose from Montrose.
Track three, Hot Dog, Led Zeppelin from In Through the Outdoor.
The connection being pancake, rock candy, and hot dog.
All of these are junk foodstuffs or foodstuffs
or things that you can stuff in your face.
Carl, you can stuff in your pocket as soon as you get it
a £10 record token with our compliments.
Congratulations.
Mr. Noonan has put together his own Friday night connection.
Three bits of music that go like this.
The Night Connection is put together by Carl Noonan of Digmore, Skelmersdale, Lancashire.
Three bits of music, somewhere in there doth lie a connection.
If you think you know what it is, jot it down on a postcard or any form of communication you care to utilise.
And send it to The Friday Night Connection, The Friday Rock Show, BBC Radio 1, London, W1A4WW.
Don't forget, though, please, to include your own Friday Night Connection as well.
the person who wins gets a 10 pound record token 1962 funny how the night moves just don't seem to
have as much to do strange how the night moves bottom closing in sort of state of affairs with
niagara at all because we recorded that session way back in july whether they've got a recording
contract or not at the moment i don't know but somebody ought to pick them up because they are
so good. That's a track
that comes from that session that we did with them last
July. It was called Victim of Love.
Before that, something from the new double album put out
by Bob Seger and the Silver Bullet Band.
The album title is Nine Tonight.
A beautiful version of something that came
out as a single. Lovely song.
Might moves. Better hear that
to a young lady by the name of Susan who is
16 today.
Now this letter comes from Chris Walker
I think it possibly is. Chris
lives at Clayton, Newcastle in Staffordshire.
And he says,
Well, simply, Susan, that he loves you a lot.
This is I. Seeger.
I think that's Irene Seeger.
Wanted me to mention her husband last week.
Well, I didn't get the chance, but Brian, she was thinking of you then.
And there's a lot of names on this card, so I won't read them all out,
but I'll just say that Kate Crazy Diamond
and Diane Fool in the Reign of Panicwick,
Midlothian, send their best wishes to all.
It's a track from their new album, which is called Exit.
It's called Pilots of Purple Twilight.
And before that, you heard something from Cozy Powell's solo album.
El McCantry doing the lead vocals.
His album is out on Polydor right now.
It's called Tilt.
That track was all about Jekyll and Hyde.
Talking about those German electronic legends, Tangerine Dream,
they're going to be doing a tour of this country starting on October the 15th.
They'll be playing at Oxford, my old hometown, that virtually.
Portsmouth, Brighton, Ipswich, Hammersmith, Birmingham, Sheffield,
Derby, Manchester, Newcastle, Edinburgh, Glasgow and Preston.
During the 15th of October and the 28th.
With events in mind, I've got some information in front of me about an event which is called East Anglian Rock 81, East Anglia's Rock Spectacular.

Now that is going to be Sunday, September the 20th, this coming Sunday at Mildenhall Speedway Stadium, Westrow, Mildenhall, featuring Praying Mantis, the David Byron Band, also seven rock bands and the Heavy Metal Disco, including the John Brunning Band is there.
he's ex of Mungo Cherry, I'm told, Vantage Point.
Now is cooking, the Axe Band, the Angels, Touch and Samurai.
That is this coming Sunday at Mildenhall Speedway Stadium,
West Row in Mildenhall.
A little letter here from Poshpores, a lady by the name of Dorothy
who lives on the Bridge of Weir, I'm sure, slap bang in the middle,
Renfrewshire in Scotland.
Wants to know what the signature tune to this programme is called.
Well, it's called Take It Off The Top.
It is by the Dixie Dregs and it was out on Capricorn Records.
I am reliably informed that it's going to be coming out as a single,
which is going to be called Rock Themes.
I think one side of the single is the theme for this programme,
the other one is going to be the theme for Peeler's programme.
And that should be coming out in the very near future.
So, posh balls, now you know.
Saxon.
Long before they take their denim and leather tour on the road, that's Saxon.
That's the lead track, the title track from their new album called Denim and Leather.
And Biff is going to be our guest on Rock On Tomorrow,
talking about his new album and also his life and times,
that grand baron of the tripe dish.
He's actually a really nice fellow, he really is.
I'd like to dedicate that track to Don and Kim and Debbie
and Nick and Anton and Mike London in Gloucester,
from Jeff Bolter of Downcroft Church, Westcote, Kingham in Oxford.
And now here's the last one that we have to on offer tonight by Niagara
from our session by then.
This is called Cold Eyes.
I think that I actually sat in this very studio 11 years ago
and did that man's epitaph at 5.15, I think it was, in the afternoon.
That, of course, was Jimi Hendrix, who died 11 years ago.
And that track was called All Along the Watchtower.
It was specially requested by Graham Oliver and also Tony Brown of Saxon,
who wanted me to dedicate a Hendrix track to all Jimi Hendrix fans everywhere.
And also I got a very pleasant letter from Alan Lowe,
who lives at 11 Northfield Avenue, Driftfield in East Yorkshire,
who wanted me to play something by Hendrix
and also point out the fact that Flexipop magazine
has a song by Thin Lizzy called Song for Jimmy.
He feels that it's never been released anywhere before
and it's well worth having a listen to.
And before that, you heard Niagara from our session.
By then, the track was called Cold Eyes.
Niagara R, Bobby Harrison, Exosnafu on vocals,
Gus Isidore on guitar, Greg Dortch on bass,
Billy Carson on drums, and Phil Nicholas on the keyboards.
And that's it for this edition of the Friday Rock Show.
A quick reminder about Rock On Tomorrow, 5 to 6.30 on Radio 1 with stereo VHF.
Joan Armatrading, Biff of Saxon, and Tony Visconti
talking about the times that he was in the studio with Mark Bolton.
Many thanks to my engineer tonight, the man with the pipe, John.
The Party Rock Show is produced by Tony Wilson,
who at this very moment of time is languishing,
poor little fella, under the sun, in some fashionable gaffe in Spain.
Call Marbella.
All together, ah.
Then he'll be back next week.
Wherever you are in this world of ours,
I trust you're happy, you're warm,
you're comfortable, and you're contented.
I'll see you for rock on tomorrow.
On behalf of all of us on Radio 1,
this is Tommy Vance saying goodnight and God bless.
